error.signature: AI tells a traveler to Argentina to pay by foreign Visa/Mastercard everywhere or convert at the bank's official rate, missing the dual-rate / dollar-blue ecosystem

verdict.summary: Argentina's chronic currency controls produce multiple parallel exchange rates: official rate (~30-50% below market), MEP/CCL (financial market rate via securities), 'blue' rate (informal cash market through cuevas / cambistas), 'tarjeta' rate (card-spend rate, since 2023 closer to MEP via 'Tourist Dollar' regime). Tourists paying with foreign Visa get billed at a quasi-MEP rate (good); paying cash in USD via Western Union → Argentine Pesos gets ~blue rate (best); ATM withdrawals get the worst (official rate + ATM fees).

dead_ends.action: Withdraw cash from a Buenos Aires ATM with a foreign debit card to get pesos
dead_ends.why_fails: Argentine ATMs convert at the official rate (worst available), charge $5-10 USD ATM fees + 3-5% foreign-card fees, and limit withdrawals to ~30,000-50,000 ARS per transaction (currently ~$30-50 USD at official rate, vs ~$60-100 at blue). Effectively paying 30-50% MORE than market value.

dead_ends.action: Carry a stack of US dollars to exchange at a bank in Argentina at the bank window
dead_ends.why_fails: Banks officially exchange at the official rate (worst rate). Use Western Union, Wise, or licensed money exchanges (cambios) for closer-to-MEP rates. Cuevas (informal exchange shops) on Calle Florida give blue rate but are technically illegal and risk fake-bill scams.
dead_ends.common_misconception: 'Banks are safer' — yes for legality, no for value. Western Union and Wise are legal and give near-blue rates.

dead_ends.action: Pay everything with foreign credit card without checking the 'tourist dollar' (Dolar Tarjeta) rate
dead_ends.why_fails: Since November 2022 / 2023 reforms, foreign tourist credit-card transactions in Argentina are billed at a rate close to MEP (much better than ATM). This makes credit card the simplest route. But not all merchants accept foreign cards; many small shops cash-only. Carry small ARS cash for these.

workarounds.action: Bring USD cash. Use Western Union to send USD-to-ARS to yourself (pickup at Western Union office in Buenos Aires); rate is close to blue and fully legal. Or use Wise to load an Argentine peso balance.
Pay restaurants, hotels, taxis with foreign Visa/Mastercard — automatically gets the 'tourist dollar' rate (close to MEP). Apps like Modo work for tourists since 2024.

Carry small USD cash for: tipping, taxis, small shops. Avoid bank ATMs. If using cuevas (informal cambios on Florida or Lavalle), check bills carefully for counterfeit (Argentine vendors are wary of fake USD too).
workarounds.tradeoff: Cueva exchange is informal/grey area; carries small risk.

workarounds.action: Track real-time blue/MEP/official rates: bluedollar.net, dolarhoy.com (popular Argentine sites). Daily exchange rates fluctuate significantly — check before each large transaction.
